refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple requirements or specify implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) is less than or equal to 150 ms, ensuring compliance with safety requirements.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency
Use a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service for teleoperation data
Incorporate predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Segment teleoperation functions into local and remote processing to reduce critical latency paths
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency

potential_sources: Tightly coupled communication and control modules causing latency spikes
Shared resources leading to contention and increased delays
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a modular design with prioritized data handling supports independence between communication and control functions, reducing coupling and enabling latency optimization.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Predictive control may be needed if latency occasionally exceeds thresholds
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including predictive control or local processing segmentation as secondary DPs if latency targets are not consistently met under variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control system including prioritized data handling to minimize latency

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for vehicle status and control feedback

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control instructions from operator to teleoperation system

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for remote control and data transmission

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains teleoperation but also many unrelated functions, causing high diffusion.
Vehicle module focuses on physical vehicle systems; teleoperation logic would diffuse significantly outside.
Highly cohesive; teleoperation control and prioritized data handling fit naturally here with minimal diffusion.
Safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not control system logic; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary that fully contains the DP of designing a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency, minimizing diffusion and coupling outside the module.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time telemetry data.
Network Link reliability and latency could impact module performance, requiring robust error handling.
Coordination with Safety Services for safety alerts may require careful interface design to avoid cross-module diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency, directly targeting the requirement for end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time telemetry data.
Coordination with Safety Services for safety alerts may require careful interface design to avoid cross-module diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module shows high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. The DP is well contained within this IM boundary, minimizing diffusion and coupling outside the module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring the teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions using the Teleoperation module interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Teleoperation IM boundary is clear and specific, with well-defined interfaces supporting the required functionality and latency constraints.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface design with the Vehicle module to manage real-time telemetry data efficiently and reduce complexity.
Develop robust error handling and latency mitigation strategies for the Network Link interface to ensure performance under variable network conditions.
Define clear coordination protocols with Safety Services to handle safety alerts without causing cross-module diffusion or coupling.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The Teleoperation module shows strong containment and low diffusion risk, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Although some interface complexity and coordination risks exist, these are manageable through recommended revisions and do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and containment is paramount, and the current level sufficiently captures the necessary detail for safe and effective implementation.

revise.why: To enhance interface design with the Vehicle module, develop robust error handling and latency mitigation strategies for the Network Link interface, and define clear coordination protocols with Safety Services to minimize diffusion and coupling risks.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the FR with respect to latency and safety requirements.
The Teleoperation IM boundary is well-defined and capable of encapsulating the DP without excessive diffusion.
Recommended interface and coordination improvements can be implemented without requiring further functional decomposition.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time telemetry data.
Coordination with Safety Services for safety alerts may cause cross-module diffusion if not carefully managed.
Network variability may impact latency, requiring robust mitigation strategies.
evidence_needed: Performance measurements of teleoperation latency under network conditions (p95 ≤ 150 ms).
Validation of interface designs with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
Verification of error handling and latency mitigation effectiveness in the Network Link interface.
